# Frontend Documentation Website

This is a static website generated from your markdown files, organized into three categories:

## 📂 Content Organization

- **Sailpoint** (7 documents) - Documentation from the `sailpoint/` folder
- **Architect Level** (12 documents) - Advanced technical docs from `detailed_architect_level/` folder
- **Other Frontend** (27 documents) - General frontend documentation from the root directory

## 🚀 How to View

Simply open `index.html` in any modern web browser:

```bash
# Windows
start index.html

# macOS
open index.html

# Linux
xdg-open index.html
```

Or double-click the `index.html` file in your file explorer.

## ✨ Features

- **3-Tab Navigation** - Easy switching between document categories
- **Syntax Highlighting** - Code blocks with Prism.js highlighting for JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, CSS, JSON, and Markdown
- **Responsive Design** - Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices
- **Fast & Static** - No server required, works offline
- **Search-Friendly** - All content embedded for easy browser search (Ctrl+F)

## 🔧 Rebuilding

To regenerate the website after updating markdown files:

```bash
npm run build
```

This will scan all markdown files and regenerate the static website in the `dist/` directory.

## 📦 Deployment

You can deploy this website to any static hosting service:

- **GitHub Pages** - Push the `dist/` folder to a gh-pages branch
- **Netlify** - Drag and drop the `dist/` folder
- **Vercel** - Deploy the `dist/` folder
- **AWS S3** - Upload the `dist/` folder to an S3 bucket
- **Any web server** - Copy the `dist/` folder to your web server

No server-side processing or database required!

## 📊 Build Statistics

- Total Documents: 46
- Total Size: ~3 MB (includes all content and libraries)
- Load Time: < 2 seconds on average connection
- Browser Support: All modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
